rationale: The cited 2011 JMR paper studies sequential self-customization and inference-driven effects on premium-option choice/spending (discrepant fluency, liking, perceived price differences), which closely overlaps the citing paper’s constructs of sequential configuration, spillover/preference updating, and downstream revenue effects.
